title: navarretia virgata subsp. floccosa
intro: Navarretia virgata subsp. floccosa, also known as the woolly stickleaf, is a rare, endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. The woolly stickleaf can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tincture form, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
